message:
fix(core): prevent CMS pathological allocation and cass truncation underflow

CountMinSketch allocation bounds:
- Add bounds constants: MIN_CMS_WIDTH (4), MIN_CMS_DEPTH (1),
  MAX_CMS_DEPTH (64), MAX_CMS_MEMORY_BYTES (64 MiB)
- Extract normalize_cms_dimensions() to centralize clamping of width
  and depth. The maximum width is derived per-depth so that
  width * depth * sizeof(u64) never exceeds the memory budget
- Apply normalization in from_error_params(), with_dimensions(), and
  with_config() — previously only from_error_params clamped minimums,
  and no path capped maximums, so CmsConfig { width: usize::MAX,
  depth: usize::MAX } would trigger a multi-exabyte allocation attempt
- Use saturating_mul in memory_bytes() to prevent overflow on
  pathological configurations that somehow bypass normalization
- Add tests: extremely small error params respect allocation budget,
  pathological config dimensions are capped before allocation

Cass redact_and_truncate fix:
- When max_len < ellipsis.len() (3), the old code computed
  budget = max_len.saturating_sub(3) = 0, truncated to 0 bytes, then
  appended "..." — producing output longer than max_len
- Now handles this edge case by truncating at a char boundary without
  appending the ellipsis

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
